TextENGNew: The sky was dyed red by the setting sun, and even the clouds took on a crimson hue.
I turned back and gazed at the substantial clock near the roof of the school building.
...It was nearly 6 PM, long past time to go home.
``Of course, nobody is doing regular club activities right before exams...``
I turned back and gazed at the field.
I made sure nobody else was around, then shrugged my shoulders and smiled.
My shadow was stretched long and thin along the ground... and somewhat vague.
I should be used to this scenery. It is what it always looks like just before I leave school... and yet, it felt strange.
``...Heeey, Minai.``
I turned back toward the voice calling out to me.
There was no need to even check who it was.
I recognized immediately that the voice came from my upperclassman.
``Nice work, Tsukada-senpai.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2365. Coming back after jogging?@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2366. It must be tough when we're so close to the competition.``
``Yeah. ...What about you?``
``I was studying at the library.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2368. I had some trouble on the midterms, so I need to recover by the end of the term... Tahaha.``
``...You liar.``
Having unconsciously been looking away, I rose my eyes upon hearing that... and then looked back at Tsukada-senpai.
He seemed angry, but also sad... It resembled he was deeply worried about me.
``Senpai...?``
``...I heard from Yabe.@k@vS20/33/VTK2_tsukada2005. You're quitting the track team?``
``...@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2371.Well, yeah.``
I scratched my cheek and answered with a bitter smile on my face.
The truth is... I decided I would tell everyone about that after school tomorrow.

So I did not want to run into any of my peers who were doing club activities, and intentionally killed time in the library to wait until later to head home... but it seems that was a completely wasted effort.
``...I'm sure you didn't come to this decision lightly.@k@vS20/33/VTK2_tsukada2008. However, could you give it just a little more thought?``
``There's no need to be so hasty.@k@vS20/33/VTK2_tsukada2010. There's still a little more time before the competition, you know?``
``Ahaha... Yabe-sensei told me the same thing.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2374. But I've already decided.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2375. As of this month... I'm quitting the track team.``
``Minai...``
``Thanks for everything, Tsukada-senpai.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2377. ...Sorry to break that promise about breaking records at the inter-high meet.``
I bowed down as I uttered those words to shake off all the thoughts running through my head.
...Even until the end, I wished to go to that competition.
But if I participated, there'd be greatly of curiosity from the audience and interviewers awaiting me, and the commotion could be a nuisance for the other contestants.
``Tragic athlete lost her parents in a fire.``
``Overcoming sadness while aiming for new records.``
...To be blunt, it hardly felt like they cared. It even came across as malicious.
Even if it is eye-catching, it'd bring me no joy to attract interest from something outside the competition.
So... I decided here and now.
It is definitely not right for me to make the other contestants uncomfortable with my lingering regrets, when they are so passionately focusing on the competition...
``...Last year's inter-high was the first and now the last time we'll be competing together.``

``It seems the boys' relay and the girls' track competition will be tough this year, so you might have a hard time handling it on your own... but please do your best.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2380. You have my support.``
``All right... I need to get going.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2382. Sorry to disturb you during practice.``
I gave Tsukada-senpai the brightest smile I could muster... then bit at my lip as I turned around and headed toward the school gate.
...I am sorry for not being more courteous.
But... please try to understand.
Today was painful enough for me just turning down the guidance counselor.
If you, too, try to stop me from quitting when I am in a state like this... I am sure I'll lose my resolve.
So now... at least for today, let me be alone...
``...@k@vS20/33/VTK2_tsukada2015.There's nobody else on the track team around.@k@vS20/33/VTK2_tsukada2016. So while you're here... why not go for the jump?``
Looking back, Tsukada-senpai had a friendly smile on his face and was pointing to the high-jump bar that was set in place a short distance away.
The coach bought a new one with the club expenses, saying it was in celebration of me reaching the national championship last year.
I was thrilled at the time, and kept on jumping not just during morning practice, but even during lunch break...
``...@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2385.However, it's getting late...``

``You worked hard for a whole year to break records, didn't you?@k@vS20/33/VTK2_tsukada2018. Consider this your homework, and give it a proper attempt.@k@vS20/33/VTK2_tsukada2019. I'll be here to act as your witness.``
...I am sure of it.
He really wished to say something else.
Don't quit.
Let's do our best together, for one more year.
Even if he did not explicitly say it... the look in Tsukada-senpai's eyes conveyed that to me.
But... he dared not say it because he knew the ``circumstances`` that led me to leave the track team.
He believed that sympathy and pity could never truly save a person... That was the kind of man Osamu Tsukada was.
changeReason: Old says 'people could only be saved with compassion and mercy' while JPN 決してないと考えている means 'believed compassion and pity could never truly save a person' — reversed meaning, opposite characterization of the person's philosophy.

TextENGNew: ...Thanks to his compassion, my eyes suddenly started feeling heated.
Right as tears were about to come spilling out, I looked up to the sky.
``You can get changed.@k@vS20/33/VTK2_tsukada2021. I still have the key to the club room.``
When I changed into my uniform and returned to the track field, the high jump bar was already set in place.
``All set up, Minai.@k@vS20/33/VTK2_tsukada2023. This height works, right?``
Gently gazing at the tick marks beside the pole... I suddenly went numb.
The bar was set one centimeter higher than my personal best... from when I won at the National Athletic Meet last year.
(He... remembered.)
Although... up until now, I'd never once managed to clear this height.
And the coach would never let me jump it, saying my form becomes clumsy when I am overly focused on it.
But now... the ban had been lifted.
I'll never have to worry about my form getting worse...
``Go on... Jump.``
Then Tsukada-senpai adjusted the bar on the poles and turned to me.
...It is almost closing time.
The chime will probably ring out by the time I have made a single jump, signaling the remaining students to leave.
I nodded to my senpai, then lined myself up with the line on the ground.
And then, I lightly inhaled and then exhaled... and at the same moment I inhaled again, I pushed on my feet and broke into a run.
I ran in a bow-shaped curve, gradually approaching the bar with light steps.
And, the moment I reached maximum speed... when I saw the shadow of the bar right beside me, I kicked on the ground and jumped back-first.
My body soared high in the air as I gazed up at the dazzling sky.
Almost on instinct, I flipped back with my legs up in the air.
In that moment of weightlessness, I sensed the sound of the bar lightly shaking... before I landed on the soft mat below me.
I turned and stared at the bar.
It was faintly quivering to and fro... but in the end, it was still on the poles... standing still.
The next moment, I heard Tsukada-senpai cheering.
``You did it, Minai!@k@vS20/33/VTK2_tsukada2026. A new personal record!``
I was delighted.
And... for some time, I just laid there motionless on the mat, with my arms and legs spread wide.
This feeling.
This joy.
Is something I'll never forget.
I held back the tears welling up inside me... while carefully reflecting on that thought.
